reviews.title: Excellent Buy to Let BTL SPV mode mortgage Products
reviews.text: I recently completed a Buy‑to‑Let (BTL) mortgage application with Saffron Building Society for my SPV limited company, and overall, my experience has been very positive. Their products for SPV landlords are excellent, and I would rate the entire process 90% strong, with 10% room for improvement. What Saffron Does Exceptionally Well: Fantastic mortgage products for SPV Limited Companies – Saffron offers one of the better ranges of specialist BTL mortgage options tailored for SPVs. Transparent and timely communication on interest rates – I really appreciate the regular updates based on inflation changes and the Bank of England base rate. This level of clarity helps landlords plan ahead with confidence. Good processing timelines – Apart from seasonal delays, their processing speed is generally reliable and well‑managed. Professional approach to business – This is what I like about Saffron the most. Their structured, process‑oriented way of working gives reassurance throughout the mortgage journey. Areas Where Improvement is Needed While the experience has been positive, there were two noticeable gaps: 1. Digital Experience – App / Portal Access Saffron’s motgage portal app and online portal are not fully user‑friendly. Although security and privacy are important—and I fully respect that—speed, accuracy, and easy digital access are equally important for today’s digital world. I still cannot access my account properly via the Android app, which reduces convenience. Improving the digital experience would definitely elevate the overall service. 2. Application Progress When Staff Are on Leave My application was processed around early to mid‑December (Dec 3rd to 15th), close to the Christmas period. If the assigned staff member is on leave, the application does not move forward. Even when follow‑up is done, the review seems superficial until the main staff member returns. Once they return, the application is re‑reviewed and sometimes additional documents are requested, causing delays. In my case, this caused nearly a two‑week delay, which added pressure from the seller and risked jeopardizing the deal. Better continuity or handover processes would prevent this. Final Thoughts Despite these two improvement areas, I genuinely like the way Saffron Building Society operates. Their product quality, communication style, and understanding of SPV landlord needs make them a strong lender in this segment. With a more user‑friendly digital platform and better application continuity during staff absence, they would deliver an even more exceptional customer experience. Overall, a solid lender with excellent potential and great service, especially for SPV landlords.

Nothing ever seems to be too much trouble for her - she has even gone above and beyond (last year) to give me ideas of which organisations to try for a new boiler / new windows that my mum has desperately needed in order to keep her warm. It is the help from Anne alone that means I would recommend the services of Saffron to others, and gladly so. Front-line / customer service staff are so important to a company and Anne seems to be a very good Ambassador of Saffron BS. Thank you!

reviews.title: Saffron Building Society Mortgage
reviews.text: I’ve had a deeply disappointing experience with Saffron Building Society’s mortgage. Their interest rates have consistently remained far above market conditions, even during periods when most lenders were offering relief. As a result, I’ve lost a significant amount of money and faced prolonged financial strain. What’s been most distressing is the lack of flexibility or fairness in their pricing—rates that feel extortionate in comparison to the broader market left us feeling trapped and anxious about our financial future. This experience has caused real emotional and financial distress for our household, and I wouldn’t recommend their mortgage products to anyone seeking stability or support from their lender.

I have used the Saffron Building Society for a number of years.  I have always found their savings products competitive and their website easy to operate.  I was very pleased to leaen The Saffron offer a loyalty bond, for customers who have stuck with them. Its very refreshing as many financial institutions offer their best rates to new customers only and not their long standing ones.  

I had reason to call today, and was dealt with by Kyle who I must say was friendly and helpful.  I am sure I will be dealing with the Saffron Building Society for many years to come. Keep up the great work.

reviews.title: Saffron BS apologised for HMRC reporting error …
reviews.text: Saffron BS apologised for incorrectly failing to report interest to HMRC on a couple of savers. They investigated, identified, and gave me a written response that included all the necessary information. They did so pretty quick too, which is pretty important given deadline for self-assessment is 31.01.26! Taking ownership is to their credit. I can't give 5 stars ... it was a Saffron error after all; but very happy to award 4 and keep using this mutual, with increased confidence  that problems are effectively sorted out in the future. Thank you, Leah, Ass. Cluster Man., for the 'sincere apology' ... it was appreciated.

reviews.title: Amateurish and not customer focused.
reviews.text:
Possibly the most amateurish organisation I've had the misfortune to deal with. My late brother had money invested with them and their customer service has been appalling e.g asking me to go into a branch when they were aware I lived 250 miles away, asking me to send documents without providing an address, asking me to complete a form that needed me to know the balance but refusing to let me know the balance without completion of the form! The sum invested was relatively low but, unlike other building societies I dealt with, they refused to release the funds without probate. Their rigid processes are not at all customer friendly.

28/01/26: Probate was just one of the issues and you said you wouldn't be reviewing your other processes.  As I promised, a complaint has now been lodged with the Ombudsman.

reviews.title: If only all customer care agents were as good as Alasdair!

Alasdair was friendly efficient, professional and incredibly patient and understanding. It was getting close to them closing and I didn’t feel at all rushed. I battled to get my account online for ages but he made it all so easy and even waited on the phone until I was up and running. If all their agents are as good then I can’t recommend Saffon enough!

reviews.title: Poor experience with Saffron
reviews.text:
I opened an account for my granddaughter which matures this year. I had not checked it, I don't think, since just after opening but tried to access it recently. On entering my details my account was immediately blocked. I spent all afternoon, spoke to four different members of staff, given four temporary passwords (two of which I later learned, did not have the correct number of characters required) and each time of trying my account was showing as blocked. I tried again for some time the following day without success. I was eventually contacted by a member of staff who did finally help me access my account and apologised for my experience. I was offered £15 as compensation and when I stated I did not think that was adequate for the stress and inconvenience I had experienced, the offer was withdrawn and I was told my complaint was not upheld! Disgusting customer care. I am definitely not happy with the experience I have had with Saffron!

Thank you for your response Saffron.  Shame your poor customer service could not have been sorted between ourselves.  And yes, I will be taking this up with the Financial Ombudsman.

Sent an email - no response, but then got the secure message response which did nor answer the question.

reviews.title: Online application system that needs sorting out
reviews.text: I have just failed to open an ISA with Saffron.  Repeated hurdles presented by their password setting and memorable question requirements.  Compliant password rejected. Then found myself timed out very rapidly and unable to get back into the application as I had no account and password... Rang Saffron. Swift and pleasant customer assistance on phone (hence  couple of stars) but was told there was nothing to do but start a new application from scratch. All they could do was close off my half finished application and either go through a new application with me on the phone or send me a form.  Their online application system password section needs sorting out.

reviews.title: I tried to open an online savings…
reviews.text: I tried to open an online savings account today, but was unable to. There were no issues until I had to enter a password and memorable word. I double checked I was following the instructions for creating a password correctly and was only using the special characters that are allowed. I changed my password in case there was something in the original that was causing the issue, but I still could not complete the application.  I started the application again in a different browser, but still hit the same brick wall!  In both browsers I was unable to view the password I had entered, which is not helpful. Another issue with the webpage is the instructions for the length of your favourite word are incorrect. You are only able to enter up to 16 characters, not 30. The issue with the password has already been reported on TrustPilot, but nothing is being done about it. Does Saffron Building Society not want new customers?

If something as basic as opening an account becomes a long, drawn-out experience, and after completing the form the system tells you it’s not possible to process and asks you to call them, it really makes you wonder what the rest of their service is like.

This experience gave us zero confidence in this building society, so we’ve decided to look elsewhere. Definitely not worth the hassle.

reviews.title: Open to everyone, not just locals
reviews.text:
I filled in a survey sent to me by email so thought I would leave a Trustpilot review too and am honestly shocked by the low overall rating. I’ve never had a problem in the over two years that I’ve been a member, other than the occasional secure message not sending (but logging into the website seems to get it to send fine.)

My latest experience was opening the Regular Saver ISA, a product that I’m a fan of but unable to get with the majority of those that offer them due to not being a local. The form was posted first class on the 8th, account opened on the 11th and passbook in my hands on the 13th. Very impressive service at any time of year, but the demand for ISAs will naturally be higher at this time making it all the more so.

Would prefer in app applications for non-ISA accounts (was website only the last time I did one) and account funding by debit card (parent is looking for a new account but doesn’t want online banking on their current account) but we can’t have everything. I have recommended Saffron to those I believe they would suit and will continue to do so.

Thank you

reviews.title: Thank you
reviews.text:
Thank you, Saffron, for getting me into this mess...and then disowning any responsibilty.
The parliamentary group took evidence from building society customers who have lost money as a result of Philips' plunge into administration. According to those present, the evidence was 'damning' with customers – nearly all elderly – pushed into inappropriate products.

Building society customers were drawn into Philips' net in the late-2010s when they were encouraged at branches to take out wills – and trusts that would protect their assets (their home and investments) from future care fees and inheritance tax.

Originally, these were provided by sister companies The Will Writing Company (TWWC) and Family Trust Corporation (FTC) – with the building societies receiving commission on each sale they made.

I hope you enjoyed your commission.

reviews.title: The Will Writing Company, Family Trust Corporation, Estate Planning Group, Philips Trust Corporation
reviews.text: Following advice from you, my mother placed her house in a trust with your partner organisation The Family Trust Corporation. She lost ownership of her home, it has cost us in excess of £14k and years of distress to rectify this. Your refusal to engage and talk about compensation, despite pressure from Kemi Badenoch MP to do so, is shameful. Anyone else affected, please contact Action Group Philips Trust on Facebook.

reviews.title: Junior savings Accounts - CANNOT LOG IN (EVER)
reviews.text: I have two savings accounts for my kids. Their paying-in system is weird - it actually goes through one giant Barclays account. Anyway, I haven't been able to actually log on to see the savings in place because I never got log in details. Trying to get through to them was long and tedious. When I did they were perfectly helpful and I did get the promised email and letter with password but I still can't log on! It's been literally weeks. The account wants to set a memorable question but you just can't get past it. I think I give up. I'm going to withdraw the £ and go to another (better) bank. reviews.text: Total waste of time, so glad I am not with this building society.  They build nothing , need to change their name to "obstructive society"  AVOID

reviews.text:
Apalling that this financial company is allowed to exist and to continue to trade.

We have been trying to close an account for over 3 months now (nothing abnormal about the request - in fact we gave up asking to OPEN a savings bond as they were so incompetent so decided to move all our monies elsewhere).  Take 2 weeks to respond, cannot get through on the phone, crazy excuses each time, and still they have our money....

Read the other 1* (zero star) reviews and stay away from this business...it will not be around for that long the way it is performing.

reviews.title: Incompetence at its worst
reviews.text:
Roll up, roll up to the most incompetent 🤡 show in town!
You'll be rolling in the aisles as you watch the hysterical antics of these clowns.
From sending actual letters in the post to a customer's accountant asking for further financial information only for it to get lost and then responding to this by replying that "We have to send it a further 3 times before we will try another way" Hilarious 😂
Watch in amazement after the customer's financial advisor complains they then reply"Oh, there was no need for us to send it in the first place!"
3 weeks wasted - side splitting fun 😂
If this isn't enough you'll fall off your seats in stitches as you watch one of the 🤡 take the role of a cleaner and accidentally pull the internet modem lead out of the wall with a broom. This then prompts Coco the 🤡 to say "You'll have to reapply because we've lost your files" 
Stop it, I can't take any more 🤣
Oh, no. The show goes on!
Through your tears of laughter you'll watch the clowns refusing to answer the phones for 3 days as they're short staffed!
Pure comedy gold 🥇
Don't waste your time and energy on this incompetent and uncaring company.

reviews.title: Out of their depth
reviews.text:
Unscrupulous and unprofessional provincial building society who are completely out of their depth in the modern market.

Avoid at all costs.

reviews.title: Calls not answered in reasonable time and poor log in
reviews.text: Like others, I opened an account online and went to log in for the first time. User ID and password were recognised, but when it came to the next section it asked for letters from my 'Memorable Answer' - without even asking me a question! I have no recollection of setting up a memorable answer, and if I had, I would have been expecting that there would be a question associated with it! Rang them as advised, as they don't send hints, but after waiting on hold for 10 minutes I gave up. The account will close in 30 days if I don't pay anything in so that's fine. It can close. It hasn't got the best interest rates now anyway - others have overtaken it since I applied last week. Not recommended.

reviews.title: Unimpressed
reviews.text: Opened a new savings account. On trying to access it online for the 1st time it didn't recognize my details/passwords for some reason (I'm sure I typed them in correctly). After x3 failed attempts the system locked the account, instructing to phone them to unlock it. Phoning gets nowhere as no one answers (tried 3x over a week waiting 10 minutes per call listening to muzak), called on Saturday morning only to get a taped message saying call back during opening hours (company info on that says they're open on Saturday mornings?), & an e.mail sent to the head office over a week ago hasn't been answered either. Received an auto/no-reply e.mail from them yesterday saying if I didn't deposit funds into the account it'll soon be closed. Am in the mood now to let that happen & go elsewhere with my £ if they're this incompetent at managing the basics of a financial's administration.

reviews.title: Just a complete waste of time

reviews.title: Saffron Building Society: Super Efficient & Fast
reviews.text: Super efficient response (and response time) on CHAT just now with Chi Cameron.  Also a helpful and quick phone response recently too. Speaking as a hearing impaired person, I am sick and tired of unacceptable call wait times and disinterested and poorly-trained staff, although to be fair they are probably poorly paid too, the whole too often equals a major waste-of-time and resources. At Saffron on CHAT, which, in general, is a massive comms boon for the deaf, there was no AI to get past either, or endless tick boxes, just an instant and accurate response to my queries. Praise where praise is due; moreover, it is in stark contrast to a recent afternoon trying to get through to the right team at Barclays and NatWest only to find the former had misinformed on product info yet again and the latter sees nothing unacceptable about 1 and half hour call wait. With the demise of High Street banking, wholesale branch closures, supporting mutuals has probably never been more needed ...

reviews.title: Not immediate or instant access.
reviews.text:
I opened a second  product with Saffron - the  E-Saver 20 described as an "immediate access savings account" and the set up was quick and easy.
Saffron state "You never know when a rainy day might come along so a savings account that gives you immediate access to your savings is always useful. As the name suggests, an Immediate Access account allows you instant access to your savings"
I assumed this would be the same as many other accounts available today where both deposits and withdrawals are completed instantly,  but found that Saffron take several hours before the  money appears in the receiving account.
Saffron need to make clear the meaning of instant/immediate access.

reviews.title: ﻿It’s not all about the headline mortgage rate
reviews.text:
This is a long review - so here’s a summary in case you don’t want to read the whole thing: high fees; very slow; pedantic; incompetent; hugely risk averse; and failed to complete on time. They took over five months to complete our simple remortgage.

We eagerly selected Saffron based on a very good rate for a five year fix RIO (Retirement Interest Only) mortgage, but here are the things that we wish we had known five months ago. Our status is that we have recently completed on a mortgage with Saffron - but that is more a measure of our persistence than anything positive about Saffron. There were many times, had the rate of this offer not been so low (while rates were rising), that we would have ditched them and gone to a ‘proper’ lender.

Fees
We were aware of most of the fees involved when we first applied for this mortgage, but not all of them. Our fees are set out below. The last item, in particular, is a ridiculous waste of money (more of this later). The valuation fee is also worth mentioning. Saffron insisted on a full valuation of the property, despite there being ample evidence of the property’s worth, and a LTV ratio of around only 11%. Ie: there was no risk to Saffron had the property been worth only a quarter of what it last changed hands for.  

In total these fees increase the effective rate of our mortgage by 1.2% over the period of the fix.  My conclusion is that Saffron places little value on, and is frivolous with, its clients’ money.

TOTAL fees of approximately £4,500 - including the following items:
Valuation fee £780
Broker’s fee (Saffron requires that you use a broker) £995
Solicitor’s fees for independent legal advice £840

Speed
Saffron has defined ’service levels’ for responding to communications and processing applications. However, it seems that rather than these service levels being the MAXIMUM period Saffron requires to respond, they reflect the period that Saffron ALWAYS takes to respond. Never, in our experience, were Saffron any quicker than the defined service level. In particular once they have all of the necessary information, they required 24 working days for the underwriting department to come up with a decision. Seriously? These days?

Pedantry
Saffron have a liking for hard copy documentation including bank statements. We had all sorts of issues getting bank statements in a format that they would accept. So if you bank with a purely online bank, that doesn’t do hard copy statements, I don’t know how you will satisfy their exacting standards of authenticity.

Incompetent
I have lost count of the number of times that documents had to be sent, and then re-sent to Saffron because they claimed not to have them. For instance, to my certain knowledge, they were sent our Powers of Attorney three times before they acknowledged they had them.

Legal Advice
Saffron’s extreme risk aversion is what drove the most annoying and unnecessary part of our encounter - the requirement that my wife and I each had to take independent legal advice. That is advice independent of EACH OTHER as well as independent of our conveyancing solicitor. We had selected the conveyancing solicitor from Saffron’s approved list on the understanding that they would be able to act for both parties as a one-stop-shop and meet all of Saffron’s requirements - and save costs. Apparently not!

Anyway, this requirement, is the most over-engineered and patronising thing I have ever encountered. We are both perfectly capable of reading the mortgage deed for ourselves. But most painful of all, was the fact that we were forced to pay for this utterly futile advice, and as expected, this process added no value whatsoever and told us nothing that we didn’t already know. 

Late Completion
Our conveyancing solicitor submitted the final documents required, in ample time, such that completion would take place on 22 December: Only to be told on the 22 December, after being repeatedly chased by our solicitor, that Saffron was unable to release funds prior to the new year. Frankly, by this stage, this was no more than I had expected - I would have been surprised if completion had happened on time. Completion finally took place on 10 January.

This failure to complete didn’t particularly matter to us - it’s a remortgage and we weren’t desperate for the cash. But what if we hadn’t been in this fortunate position, or we were moving house? We might have ended up in default, or a transaction chain might have collapsed. You couldn’t trust Saffron with any transaction which is time-critical, and it would be foolhardy to attempt to do so.

I hope that this cautionary tale makes you think twice about selecting Saffron as your lender.

Nicola was the first person I spoke to and I am so sorry to the second whose name I didn't catch.

reviews.title: I would leave 0 stars if I could.
reviews.text:
After waiting weeks and weeks and sending endless amounts of paperwork and documents, we were rejected at the final stage today leaving a chain of buyers/sellers frustrated.

To be rejected on a car finance is ridiculous, my wage alone covers the mortgage payments plus extra and my partners wage is quadruple  plus extra of what my wage is and we have no dependents.

I cannot believe we have wasted so much time waiting around for this company when we could have gone elsewhere and have now lost our dream house as its just all taken far too long that the sellers want to put it back on the market.

reviews.title: DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME GETTING A…
reviews.text:
DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME GETTING A MORTGAGE WITH THIS COMPANY❗️❗️❗️❗️

reviews.title: I love my building society
reviews.text:
I love my building society. I've been with them since 2016 and have recommended them to my friends ever since. 

It took me years to find a lender who I feel actually treats you as an individual. I don't think I'll ever have to go anywhere else for a mortgage.

reviews.title: Quick, efficient and friendly service
reviews.text: I recently opened an online regular saver with Saffron BS and have been most impressed with their speedy service. I rang this morning as I had accidently made the same payment into my account twice and I got straight through to a very helpful woman who told me that the second payment will be returned to my nominated bank account by the end of today. A very competitive savings rate of 2% for depositing £50/month is offered and is certainly more than many of the bigger banks and building societies offer. Keep up the great work.

reviews.title: Dreadful
reviews.text: Dreadful. I setup a savings account for my son last year, attracted by the good savings rate. However, when it comes to withdrawing the money they will only provide a cheque. I'm sure this can only have been done deliberately to make it difficult to withdraw the money. The new savings account I have setup for him will not accept cheques and I am unable to pay it into my own account. This has caused a lot of trouble and I'm still not able to access the money.

reviews.title: Absolutely dreadful
reviews.text: Absolutely dreadful. They accepted my application for an expat mortgage and happily took my application fee and then the survey fee. Only after completing the survey did they then turn round and say I needed a UK income - for an EXPAT mortgage! Promised to refund all my fees and then only refunded part of the fees. Is this even legal?? Avoid at all costs.

reviews.title: Brilliant service in dealing with…
reviews.text: Brilliant service in dealing with bereavement. They called to let us know how things were progressing and dealt with everything in 10 working days. High standard professional service and didn’t have the unnecessary bureaucracy of the high street banks.

reviews.title: Do not apply for a mortgage here.
reviews.text: I will be honest, if I could rate less than 1 I would. I am a first time buyer and have been trying to buy for quite some time. I understand that due to covid things take a little longer, but to wait 3 MONTHS to get an answer is a little too much on the ridiculous side for me. Not only that to then hear that the mortgage has been rejected due to the property being on a main road (is that not 90% of houses in this country) it’s just appalling and disgusting to be honest. Don’t even think about going to this bank for a mortgage a waste of time and now the whole sale might fall through. -10 out of 5 for me.

reviews.title: Stay away from time waste lender
reviews.text: I applied for BTL , after lot of document asking from this lender at various stages after 6 weeks they felt my portfolio is growing too fast and they decided not lend. How shame is on under writer wasting so much of time and valuation fee come to this decision. I secured mortgage from Paragon who is happy to lend for the same property. Never in life again with this lender as I felt they are Time Wasters and nuisance for the business as well.

reviews.title: I had the most irresponsible response…
reviews.text: I had the most irresponsible response ever from Saffron this morning following my application to open an instant access esavings account. For some unknown reason, they could not validate my nominated bank account. Odd, bearing in mind I have banked with Nationwide for over 25 years, but ok, I have recently moved house. I wanted to deposit the proceeds, as I was due to be in rental for around a year or so. However, I was requested to supply Saffron with a copy of my bank statement, by either popping in to a Saffron branch, or by getting it certified, which would entail obtaining the signature of an approved individual, who would clarify that yes, the bank statement was mine. All for 0.5% interest rate online! Both options involved both disproportionate effort, or risk to health due to the inadequacies of their systems. So I stuck it in a Hargreaves Lansdown active savings Account, and recommend you all do the same. For a few quid every £100,000 it isn’t worth the effort. I did have more than that to invest, but obviously they don’t need the business 😂.

reviews.title: No point working with this company
reviews.text: No point working with this company, you will not get anything from them. The company gave me a deadline to submit documents in order to get a mortgage from them. As the deadline approached, they made sure they were unavailable to take phone calls or even answer emails and then they said that I had missed the deadine. A complete and utter waste of time and effort dealing with these shady operators.

reviews.title: Worst company I have ever dealt with…
reviews.text: Worst company I have ever had the misfortune of dealing with. Applied for a mortgage, was approved in principle but seller pulled out. Because I had approval in principle, I found another property and put an offer in. Saffron would not honour the AIP and did a whole new application and wanted all fees again. Took weeks to get AIP again but valuation was then declined because the flat that I wanted to buy was near arcades, pubs and shops. Now back to the drawing board after weeks of being messed around. Would not have even given 1 star for Saffron but couldn't add review without 1. Awful, awful mortgage lender!

reviews.title: Tried to open a savings account online
reviews.text: Tried to open a savings account online. After one month I telephoned their customer services and was told they have my application and they would respond soon. Another month goes by and I still haven’t heard from Saffron Walden. I contact customer services again who now can’t find my application. I was advised to open the account at a branch. I go to the branch with my identification etc., and apply for the account. I was told I should get a letter advising of my account number in the next few days. Two weeks later I still haven’t had a letter so I contact customer services who advised my application had been refused. They said they don’t have to give a reason. To say I am appalled by the very poor service is an understatement. I would never recommend this building society.

reviews.title: The best savings provider I've used.
reviews.text: The very best savings provider I've been with in terms of putting the customer first, which all claim to do but only 1% actually do. I've saved for 25 years with various providers, moving around when bonus deals expire, that sort of thing. They say/said they might not always be top of the best rates but would be very near the top, a pledge they've kept. They've always given me plenty of notice when rates are to change, and allowed me to transfer to higher rates in instances where others would restrict access to new customers. Reluctantly the N S and I easy access rate is so much better than anyone else can compete with that I rang them to close my account. I was treated in the same exemplary fashion as if I was opening an account. Bigger providers could learn a lesson in customer service.

reviews.title: Very happy. Great customer service.
reviews.text: I've been with them a couple of years.  Having a few technical probs with the online side of things but that's probably more my incompetence than a reflection on them.  Customer service over the phone and in branch are brilliant and their products are pretty good too.  :)

reviews.text: Abysmal response time. The entire purpose of an online chat is the speed of reply and relay of information. It took 35 minutes to get 4 messages out of the operator, while I replied within a minute to each message.

First they said they couldn't verify dads account number electronically And asked us to send a statement, now they keep telling us they dont have the statement which they signed for 6 days ago seems like they are trying to hold onto the money, as i managed to verify my dads account electronically in a matter of seconds when i had to transfer some money to him. We are now dealing with head office so lets hope they sort it otherwise we will be contacting the financial ombudsman. 11/06/20 today having sent them the proof they have found the paperwork apparently the post office delivered it to another branch, they now say they will transfer the funds by tomorrow morning.

reviews.text:
Must be the best building society in UK!  We've been with the Saffron for quite a few years now, so can recommend them 100%.  Unlike the Nationwide they haven't grown too big, probably for this reason, the customer service is excellent. Well done to you all at Saffron.
